name: Screen Shake
description: A short camera displacement used to emphasize impact, danger, or power.
design_notes: Screen Shake should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For camera mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use screen shake as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect screen shake to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels oriented and confident because the game frames what matters.

parameters.name: follow_lag
parameters.description: How far behind the target the camera is allowed to feel.

parameters.name: framing_offset
parameters.description: Offset used to keep the important subject away from dead center.

parameters.name: transition_speed
parameters.description: How quickly the camera blends to the new behavior.

edge_cases: Screen Shake is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Screen Shake overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Screen Shake runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Screen Shake state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Screen Shake plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Screen Shake produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Screen Shake strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Screen Shake becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Screen Shake with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Screen Shake as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Screen Shake with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Screen Shake as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.

example_games.title: Nuclear Throne
example_games.note: Uses shake to emphasize weapon impact.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses restrained shake to sell impacts and transitions.
